chunk_summary: The National VAS Prophylaxis Schedule in India outlines age-based vitamin A supplementation for children, emphasizing correct spacing, documentation, and safe administration to prevent missed doses or adverse effects.
learning_objectives: Understand the age-based dosing schedule for Vitamin A supplementation in children under 5 years in India.
Learn the importance of documentation and spacing in Vitamin A prophylaxis to avoid missed or double doses.
Recognize safe administration practices and potential adverse effects of high-dose Vitamin A.
key_facts: Vitamin A supplementation starts at 9 months with 1 mL (1 lakh IU) alongside MR vaccination.
Second dose is given at 16–18 months with 2 mL (2 lakh IU), followed by doses every 6 months until 5 years (total 9 doses).
Documentation is critical: verify the last dose date and schedule the next dose at least 6 months later.
Avoid repeat high-dose prophylaxis based on recall alone; retrieve records from local sub-centres if unavailable.
Administration is oral; transient nausea or vomiting may occur but repeated closely spaced doses increase adverse effects.
reference_quote: The first dose is given at 9 months (1 mL containing 1 lakh IU) typically alongside measles-rubella (MR) vaccination, followed by a second dose at 16–18 months (2 mL containing 2 lakh IU).
document_key_topics: Age-based Vitamin A supplementation schedule in India
Documentation and spacing practices for VAS
Safe administration and adverse effects of high-dose Vitamin A
